metadata.title:
WILL SOON OPEN LAST


OF GOOD indian LAND

pages.raw_text:
WASHINGTON, D. c., Aug. 81
One of the best of the few remain.
ing tracts of indian lands Is soon
to be thrown open for settlement
This tract is located near Lawton,
In Comanche. Caddo and Kiowa
counties, Oklahoma. 1t comprises
something over 100000 acres, and
Is known locally as the "big pas.
ture."


The big pasture consists OF lands
originally set aside for grazing the
cattle of the Kiowa. Apache and
Comanche indians, and 1t Is to the
survivors Of these tribes that the
proceeds of the sale will go-or
rather 1t Is to them that the inter-
est at 4 per cent will go, the prin-
cipal being held by the interior de-
partment in trust.


The big pasture lands are not to
he had free, or even for a nominal
price. The plan on which they will
be disposed of is a combination of
settlement and sale. The law pro-
vides that none of the lands can be
sold for less than IS an acre, and
1t is the plan of the department to
sell to the highest bidder. But
whether the bidding will be at auc-
ton or by sealed bids has not been
determined.


Neither has the date of the sale
been determined; only that it will
come some time before Dec. g.
probably about Nov. IS.


OF the half million acres $4,000
acres are woodland. The rest is
rolling pasture, exceedingly rich
and well suited to all forms of agri.
culture. 1t is expected that some of
the land will sell for as high as s25
and even $40 an acre.


The law provides that purchasers
or these lands must be actual set
Tiers In order to secure title. The
least time man can live upon the
lands and secure title Is IA months.
ana the normal time is five years.


Some of the lands are now leased
and these are to be sold subject to
the terms of these leases. In addi.
